threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: When you call it 'the Underground' instead of just 'the Tube' and look lost on a bus route. Everyone knows the 55 goes the long way.
Saw a guy serenading the night bus crew with a ukulele at 2 AM in Brixton. The driver actually joined in for a verse, like a mini gig on wheels.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a busker serenading a pigeon on the corner of Hermannstraße. Only in Neukölln do even the birds get a show.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you chill by the canal while a spontaneous techno party erupts on a boat, complete with strobe lights and DJ set-up. Pure Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they instinctively avoid eye contact with the guy hawking incense on 16th. Tourists always stop to chat.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni ride home last night, and a street performer turned the bus into a concert. Only in the Tenderloin do you see that kind of spontaneous vibe!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals eat tapas at every meal. Most of us prefer home-cooked meals and save tapas for special nights out with friends.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer in Gràcia by their habit of trying to order 'café con leche' at 3 PM—locals know it's 'café amb llet' time for breakfast only.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You know you’re new when you wait at the bus stop and still think it’s a ‘really long time’ if it’s more than 5 minutes. Welcome to Hackney!
Eerily quiet on the N137 last night—only one guy arguing with the driver about not stopping at Loughborough Junction. Classic London.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw an old man feeding pigeons in front of a hip café while a group of kids played futsal nearby. Classic Neukölln mix.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find a late-night kebab shop that also doubles as a gallery for local artists. It's a feast for the stomach and the eyes.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: When they complain about the Civic Center BART station closure but can't even name the last mural on 24th, you know they're just passing through.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading Muni riders with a ukulele at Civic Center. Even the driver was tapping along. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all Gaudí and hipster cafés, but our true charm lies in the tiny, crumbling squares where locals still gather for genuine chats.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll see them staring at maps on the street corners, lost in the grid and oblivious to the locals weaving past at a brisk pace.
